operating_state: Total revenue increased $1.8 billion or 13% driven by strong travel demand and 17% capacity expansion, with adjusted revenue rising 19% excluding refinery sales. Operating income improved $972 million to $2.5 billion as revenue growth substantially outpaced cost increases. American Express partnership generated $1.7 billion in cash sales, up 22% year-over-year, reflecting continued strength in premium travel segments.
cost_structure: Total operating expenses increased $782 million or 6% primarily from higher capacity and increased employee costs including wages and profit sharing, partially offset by lower fuel expense. Cost per available seat mile decreased 9% due to fuel savings and capacity leverage, while non-fuel unit costs rose 2.4%. Operating leverage remained positive with revenue growth significantly exceeding expense growth.
material_events: Board elected Vasant Prabhu as director in April, appointing him to Finance and Safety committees. Michael Spanos was named Chief Operating Officer effective June 12, bringing experience from Six Flags CEO role and 25-year PepsiCo career. Board declared quarterly dividend of $0.10 per share payable August 7 to shareholders of record July 17.
macro_exposures: No material changes from previously disclosed risk factors were reported during the quarter. Standard airline industry exposures to fuel costs, weather disruptions, regulatory changes, and economic conditions remain unchanged from prior periods.
forward_outlook: Company held June 2023 Investor Day presenting strategic direction and long-term outlook. Management maintained focus on capacity expansion and operational excellence while continuing debt reduction with $1.8 billion in debt and lease repayments during the quarter. Free cash flow generation of $1.1 billion supports ongoing capital allocation priorities.
uncertainty_profile: Liquidity position of $8.8 billion provides operational flexibility amid standard industry uncertainties. Debt covenant compliance requirements continue following pandemic-related borrowing. Technology system security and data privacy regulatory compliance remain ongoing operational priorities without specific new exposures disclosed.
